title: CGELBank core category/function codebook
description: A narrow registry package for CGELBank's core representation of English syntax: node categories, incoming-edge functions, and projection-spine conventions. It summarizes the codebook-like layer documented in the CGELBank manual and the published annotation study, not the full CGELBank annotation system.

annotation_target.unit: tree node and incoming edge in a CGELBank-style constituency tree
annotation_target.definition: Each relevant constituent node receives one category label, and most non-root nodes also receive a function label for the relation to the parent constituent. The package covers the core distinction between categories and functions plus the projection requirements documented for CGELBank.
inclusion_criteria: The item is a lexical node, phrasal node, non-headed category, gap terminal, or incoming-edge function in a CGELBank-style tree.
The decision concerns the core category/function/projection layer rather than a phenomenon-specific construction analysis.
The analysis is intended to follow CGELBank documentation derived from CGEL and its companion materials.
exclusion_criteria: Fine-grained construction policies not needed for the core category/function inventory.
Automatic conversion from UD/PTB into CGELBank where conversion-specific heuristics rather than core annotation decisions are at issue.
Claims about speaker cognition, acceptability, or processing.
LLM-generated annotations without a separate instrument-validation record.
source_domains: CGELBank documentation, Reynolds, Schneider, and Arora 2022/2023
Reynolds, Arora, and Schneider 2023 interannotator study on English Web Treebank sentences

projection_target.supported_question: Can trained annotators and validators produce comparable CGELBank-style English syntax trees under a declared category/function formalism?
projection_target.target_description: The target inference is consistency of CGELBank-style structural annotation for English treebanking, especially tree comparison, validator effects, and parser/treebank conformance against a declared representation.

registers: English Web Treebank sentences in the published IAA study
CGELBank naturally occurring sentences across documented project splits
corpora: English Web Treebank subset used for the reported IAA study
scope.period: present-day English as represented in CGELBank documentation and the 2023 annotation study
exclusions: This package does not encode the whole CGELBank manual.
This package does not validate LLM annotation.
This package does not claim coverage for all English constructions.

input_assumptions: The input is tokenized for CGELBank-style tree annotation, including gap terminals where relevant.
Annotators have access to the CGELBank manual and CGEL/SIEG2 for substantive construction analysis.
The task is structural annotation of English syntax, not semantic role labelling or dependency-only annotation.
Root nodes receive category labels; most other nodes receive both a category and an incoming-edge function.

labels.name: Node category
labels.definition: The constituent type assigned to a tree node, including lexical categories, phrasal projections, non-headed categories, and gap terminals as documented for CGELBank.
positive_cues: The label identifies what kind of constituent the node is.
Lexical categories normally project corresponding phrasal categories, subject to CGELBank's documented exceptions.
Non-headed categories such as Coordination and GAP are represented as categories rather than functions.
negative_cues: A grammatical relation to the parent is a function label rather than a node category.
A discourse role, semantic role, or dependency-only relation is outside this core category layer.

examples.expected_label: DP and Nom phrasal projections over D and N lexical nodes
examples.rationale: CGELBank documents that nodes are not omitted in unary branches, so a determinative and noun project the relevant phrase levels.

anti_examples.rationale: Head is a function label, not a node category.

labels.name: Incoming-edge function
labels.definition: The grammatical function of a constituent in relation to its parent, displayed on the node for convenience but applying to the incoming edge.
positive_cues: The label states the constituent's role in the larger phrase or clause.
Core examples include Head, Obj, Subj, Mod, Comp, Det, Coordinate, Marker, Supplement, and Flat.
Function labels are distinct from category labels even when the ordinary-language terms are similar.
negative_cues: A constituent type such as NP, VP, Nom, Clause, D, or N is a category label.
A lexical item by itself is not a function label.

examples.expected_label: Head for eat; Obj for apples
examples.rationale: The verb heads the VP and the NP bears the object function.

anti_examples.rationale: NP is a phrasal category rather than a function.

labels.name: Projection conformance
labels.definition: Whether the tree respects CGELBank's core projection-spine requirements, including phrasal projection of lexical categories and required Nom/VP layers where documented.
positive_cues: Lexical nodes project the corresponding phrasal category unless a documented exception applies.
NPs are headed by Nom or another NP level; clauses are headed by VP or another clause level.
Modifiers are layered rather than freely combined with complements at the same level unless a documented exception applies.
negative_cues: CGEL print examples may omit unary nodes; CGELBank generally does not.
A semantically plausible dependency does not override the documented projection-spine requirement.

examples.rationale: The CGELBank representation includes the DP and Nom layers rather than omitting unary branches.

anti_examples.text: NP directly over D and N in the CGELBank target representation
anti_examples.source: Derived contrast from CGELBank documentation's unary-branching policy
anti_examples.rationale: CGELBank documents that nodes are never omitted in this kind of unary branch.

labels.name: Outside core scope
labels.definition: The decision belongs to a construction-specific, conversion-specific, semantic, psycholinguistic, or LLM-instrument layer rather than the core category/function/projection package.
positive_cues: The decision requires a detailed construction-specific policy not encoded in this core package.
The question asks whether an LLM can reproduce the analysis, which requires a separate instrument-validation record.
The question concerns downstream theoretical interpretation rather than tree representation.
negative_cues: The question can be resolved by assigning a node category, incoming-edge function, or core projection-spine relation.

decision_rules.title: Separate category from function
decision_rules.rule: Assign category labels to nodes and function labels to incoming parent-child relations; do not use a function label as a constituent category or a constituent category as an edge function.

decision_rules.title: Preserve CGELBank projection spines
decision_rules.rule: Represent documented CGELBank phrasal projections, including unary branches, unless a documented CGELBank exception applies.

decision_rules.title: Check headedness and non-headed categories
decision_rules.rule: Headed phrases require the documented Head relation; Coordination and GAP are treated as non-headed categories where the manual says so.

decision_rules.title: Scope out construction-specific policies
decision_rules.rule: If the annotation decision depends on a construction-specific chapter or a later CGELBank policy not encoded here, mark it outside-core-scope and cite the relevant policy rather than extending this core package silently.

hard_cases.description: The manual notes that CGEL's description of adjuncts leaves room for uncertainty between modifiers and supplements.
hard_cases.default_action: Use the CGELBank manual's default: ordinary time, place, manner, condition, and reason adjuncts are modifiers unless clearly presented as addenda.

hard_cases.description: Pre-head dependents in nominals may be complement-like under some descriptions but are treated by CGELBank as modifiers in the documented core policy.
hard_cases.default_action: Use the CGELBank manual's modifier treatment for the core package; cite the construction-specific policy if a case falls outside the simple rule.

hard_cases.description: CGEL print trees may omit phrasal nodes in unary branches, while CGELBank normally represents them.
hard_cases.default_action: Preserve the CGELBank phrasal node unless a documented exception applies.

hard_cases.description: Multiword names, compounds, and related expressions may require Flat or Compounding rather than ordinary headed modification.
hard_cases.default_action: Treat as outside-core-scope unless the needed Flat/Compounding policy is explicitly in the current package.

hard_cases.description: Gaps and displaced material were a lower-agreement zone in the published IAA study.
hard_cases.default_action: Use the CGELBank manual and validator; do not infer high reliability for gap analysis from the core category/function package alone.

uncertainty.interpretation: Uncertainty records whether a decision is directly supported by this package, flagged by validation, requires expert adjudication, or belongs outside the core package.

instrument_assumptions: Primary instruments are trained human annotators using the CGELBank manual and validator.
The package may be used to test parsers or LLMs only after a separate instrument-validation record is created.
Published IAA results validate trained-human use of the CGELBank framework, not model-independent automatic annotation.
forbidden_dependencies: Do not infer CGELBank labels from Universal Dependencies labels without a declared conversion layer.
Do not treat CGEL print-tree abbreviations as CGELBank tree requirements when the manual specifies a fuller representation.
Do not treat published human IAA as validation of LLM output.

target_metrics: tree edit distance based labelled flex F1
validator effect on agreement with adjudicated trees

supported_inferences.claim: The core CGELBank category/function formalism can support consistent trained-human annotation of English syntax trees.
supported_inferences.scope: English sentences like those in the reported CGELBank EWT IAA sample and related documented CGELBank splits.
supported_inferences.evidence_required: Published IAA metrics, adjudication protocol, validator effects, and manual-backed annotation decisions.

supported_inferences.claim: The codebook can support treebank/parser conformance checks against a declared CGELBank representation.
supported_inferences.scope: Core category, function, projection-spine, and validator-facing structural decisions.
supported_inferences.evidence_required: A valid tree format, a validator run, and comparison against adjudicated CGELBank-style trees.

unsupported_inferences: No claim that this package encodes all of CGELBank.
No claim that CGELBank exhausts English syntax.
No claim about speaker cognition or processing.
No claim that LLMs can annotate CGELBank reliably without separate validation.
No claim that UD/PTB labels can be losslessly converted into CGELBank categories and functions from this package alone.
scope_conditions: Annotators must use the CGELBank manual or equivalent policy source.
Tree comparison should report the metric used, since strict, flex, gap, and full-tree scores answer different questions.
Validator warnings must be treated as prompts for review, not automatic proof of error.
Construction-specific extensions should be versioned rather than silently folded into the core package.
failure_zones: Cases where CGELBank deliberately diverges from CGEL's printed representation.
demotion_triggers: New annotators cannot reproduce the category/function separation after training.
Validator warnings concentrate in a structural zone not represented in the package.
Full-tree exact match is treated as the sole reliability criterion despite known partial-agreement structure.
A model/parser is evaluated against this package without a separate instrument-specific validation record.

provenance.notes: Source-grounded micro-package based on the local CGELBank documentation PDF and the 2023 LAW paper. It summarizes a narrow core layer rather than the full annotation manual.
